Notes: Roast/rest/cool is fixed for one oven load; labor scales with washing, panning, peeling, portioning, labeling, and added cleanup.
SOP expectation says peeling and portioning is 45-60 minutes for 2 beet batches with 2 people; the tracker shows larger runs move faster than a strict per-bag peel rate.
Use one oven-load fixed time if the oven can hold all pans; add another fixed roast/rest/cool block only if a second oven round is needed.
Large runs are mostly limited by peeling and portioning flow, not roasting.
Plan extra people on peeling/portioning if the elapsed day is too long.
Regular 14-bag beet runs should plan around 8h 40m, not 14h.
If sink, pan, or cart space is tight, stage washing/panning while the first pans are roasting.
Roasting should not be multiplied by bag count unless oven capacity forces multiple oven rounds.
This is close to the tracker shape: 1.5h washing/panning + 4h bake block + 6h peeling/portioning.
